evals.expected_output: Full stock analysis report for Microsoft with all template sections populated, current data, valuation analysis, and clear BUY/SELL/HOLD recommendation with conviction level
expectations: Report file is saved as a markdown file at the specified output path
Report contains a Recommendation section with explicit BUY, SELL, or HOLD action
Report contains Conviction level (High, Medium, or Low)
Report contains a Time Horizon for the recommendation
Report has an Executive Summary section
Report has a Quality Metrics Dashboard with Piotroski F-Score, Altman Z-Score, ROIC, and FCF Yield
Report has a Valuation Summary section with P/E, EV/EBITDA, or P/S multiples
Report includes an intrinsic value estimate or margin of safety calculation
Report has a Scenario Analysis with bull, base, and bear cases
Report has an Options Analysis section
Report contains actual current MSFT price data (not fabricated)
Report has a Sources section with references
Report includes comparison to market benchmarks (S&P 500 or Nasdaq)
Options Analysis contains numeric IV Rank or IV Percentile values (not N/A or unavailable)
Valuation Summary includes historical averages for comparison (not all N/A)
Report states what would invalidate the thesis (invalidation criteria)

evals.prompt: What do you think about GOLD (Barrick Gold)? I've been watching gold prices climb and wondering if miners are a good play here.
evals.expected_output: Analysis that identifies GOLD as a commodity producer, leads with gold commodity price analysis, calculates AISC margin, explains miner leverage, and contextualizes recommendation to the gold thesis
expectations: Report file is saved as a markdown file at the specified output path
Report identifies or classifies GOLD/Barrick as a commodity producer or miner
Report discusses gold commodity price before or prominently alongside company analysis
Report mentions AISC (all-in sustaining cost) or production cost/breakeven
Report explains miner leverage to underlying commodity with quantified amplification (e.g. a 5% move in gold means X% in miners)
Report contains a Recommendation section with explicit BUY, SELL, or HOLD action
Report contains Conviction level (High, Medium, or Low)
Report has a Valuation Summary section with actual numeric historical averages (not all N/A)
Report discusses gold price trend or outlook as part of the thesis
Report has a Sources section with references
Recommendation rationale connects back to the gold commodity thesis
Report calculates explicit per-unit profit margin (spot price minus AISC in $/oz or equivalent)
Report has a dedicated commodity price section or heading (not just mentioning gold in passing)

evals.prompt: I'm looking at selling puts on SOFI - IV seems elevated. Do a full screen on it.
evals.expected_output: Full analysis with substantive options section including IV rank/percentile, evaluation of put-selling strategy, specific guidance, plus complete fundamental/valuation backdrop
expectations: Report file is saved as a markdown file at the specified output path
Report has an Options Analysis section that is more than 20 lines
Report mentions IV Rank or IV Percentile with numeric values in the options analysis
Report specifically evaluates the put-selling strategy the user asked about
Report recommends for or against selling puts with reasoning tied to IV environment
Report contains a Recommendation section with explicit action (BUY/SELL/HOLD or options strategy)
Report contains Conviction level (High, Medium, or Low)
Report has a Valuation Summary section with multiples
Report has fundamental analysis providing context for the options play
Report has a Sources section with references
Report contains actual current SOFI price data (not fabricated)
Report presents multiple named options strategies or strike/expiry alternatives
Report includes risk factors or invalidation criteria specific to the options position
